name: User nudges a stale quote
description: A quote sat in 'sent' for 11 days with no opens. /quotes flags it as stale; user clicks 'Nudge' to send a friendly follow-up.

persona.name: Diego Martínez
persona.context: Friday review. Doesn't want a quote to die quietly.

preconditions: Quote 'Garcia patio' sent 11 days ago, 0 opens
Customer has email + phone on file; preferred channel = email

steps.expect: Garcia card is in the 'stale' stage with an explicit 'Nudge' affordance
Modal/sheet with a pre-written friendly message ('Hi Garcia family — circling back on the patio quote...') editable
Card moves out of stale (or marker shows 'Nudged 1m ago'); the activity feed records the nudge

emails.trigger: user clicks Send in nudge modal
emails.subject: Quick follow-up — your patio quote
emails.expect: Email is from Diego's name, not 'Paperwork Monsters'; warm tone; CTA repeats the quote total and Accept button

evaluationCriteria.check: Stale stage has visual decay (dim, muted) — but not depressing
User can nudge without composing from scratch; default copy doesn't feel canned
Quote can only be nudged once per 48h to avoid spamming customers
Nudge button has an accessible name including the client name ('Nudge Garcia patio')
